title: Warm Charred Egyptian Oats Pilaf
description: A comforting, charred oat pilaf inspired by Egyptian flavors, enriched with chickpeas and tahini for a creamy finish.
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium‑high heat with 1 tablespoon olive oil until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and minced garlic, sauté until fragrant and lightly charred, about 3‑4 minutes.
Stir in the diced carrot and continue to cook until slightly softened, about 2 minutes.
Add the rolled oats, chickpeas, and sauté for another 2 minutes, allowing the oats to toast and develop a subtle char.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until the oats are tender and the liquid is absorbed, about 15 minutes.
Stir in the tahini, lemon juice, salt, and pepper, mixing until creamy and well combined.
Remove from heat, garnish with chopped parsley, and serve warm.
